Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 to find all accounts where Account Manager is "Catalina Dubois." Key discoveries: 3 active client accounts (Canopy Health recAcct000000001, PivotPoint Labs recAcct000000004, NorthWind Technologies recAcct000000064) plus multiple cold outreach accounts (Veridian Software, Clearpath Financial, Arcadia Consulting, etc.). NorthWind record shows Status "Active" and notes dated Feb 28 with no mention of the churn crisis.
2. Search Airtable Relocations table (tblRelocations01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 to check active or in-progress relocations under Catalina's client accounts. Key discoveries: NorthWind has Raj Patel (Chicago to Denver, chain-of-custody breach) and Emilia Cruz (Chicago to Denver, piano damage) in progress. Canopy Health has Q2 wave (Dr. Kovacs Nashville to Toronto, Marcus Webb Detroit to Chicago, Tyler Okonkwo Philadelphia to Denver). PivotPoint Labs has Nadia Okonjo (H-1B visa) and Diego Castaneda (CNC equipment) dual relocation.
3. Search CRM for deals tied to Catalina's accounts using crm_search_deals with query related to "NorthWind" or "Canopy" or "PivotPoint." Key discoveries: deal_northwind_won ($60,000, closedWon), deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 ($14,700, contractSent with description showing chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, Pam's escalation, PathFinder competitor). Canopy and PivotPoint deals also exist.
4. Search CRM engagements for Catalina's accounts using crm_list_engagements with company_ids for Catalina's clients. Key discovery: engagement-terraform-replyall-incident documenting the April 22 reply-all pricing leak where Catalina accidentally exposed MoveOps' margin floor and tiered discount structure to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital. Elena assigned the account to Mina and ordered Catalina to apologize.
5. Search emails for complaints or escalations related to Catalina's accounts using search_emails with query related to "Catalina" or "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation." Key discoveries: Pam Kowalski's formal final warning to David (bypassing Catalina), citing chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and the April 14 recovery promise that was never delivered. Also: Catalina's reply-all of internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform.
6. Search Slack for discussions about Catalina's performance using conversations_search_messages with query related to "Catalina" or "redistribute" or "NorthWind" or "overload." Key discoveries: David's #executive (C004) post asking Elena to redistribute Catalina's accounts (April 16), Elena's reply "Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now" (April 16 thread in C004), David's April 25 follow-up in #executive saying he's stuck and "she told me in #operations she's at capacity," Catalina going silent on David's vendor exposure mapping request (discoverable via Linear comment linear_comment_3b5c18789606 from April 15 — David asked all AMs for client exposure input, Mina and Emeka responded, Catalina did not).
7. Search emails for Catalina's Q1 performance data using search_emails with query related to "Q1 performance" or "AM scorecard" or "margin analysis." Key discoveries: David's Q1 AM Performance Overview email showing Catalina's metrics: $64,800 revenue, 32% avg margin, 5.5/10 CSAT, 2 escalations, 0% repeat rate. Also Alejandro's Q1 margin analysis showing the NorthWind deal had $4,200 in unexpected costs dropping effective margin to 28%, and the Lakeline Logistics $78,200 deal loss (deal_lakeline_lost, closedLost).
8. Search Linear for open tickets related to Catalina's accounts using linear_list_issues with query related to "NorthWind" or "Canopy" or "PivotPoint." Key discoveries: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (P1, assigned to David Chen — describes Pam's final warning, chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz callback miss, Catalina's broken April 14 promise, PathFinder competitor mention, stale CRM). Also issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind "DOT Docs — Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS" (state: done — but Slack comments reveal the chain-of-custody log was never actually signed despite this issue being marked complete). For Canopy: Dr. Mehta executive relocation workstream tickets exist but are assigned to Mina/Fatimah, not Catalina. For PivotPoint: no open tickets found.
9. Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "David Chen." Key email: david.chen@moveops.com.
10. Update stale Airtable Client Account records (via airtable_update_records) on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblClientAccts01. Key update: NorthWind Technologies (recAcct000000064) Status from "Active" to "At Risk" and Notes updated to reflect Pam Kowalski's final warning, PathFinder competitor threat, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and Catalina's broken April 14 recovery promise.
11. Update CRM deal stage (via crm_update_deal_stage) for NorthWind's at-risk deal to reflect the current churn status, and create an engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) summarizing the situation across Catalina's accounts.
12. Create a Linear tracking issue (via linear_create_issue) for the account review documenting Elena's assessment of Catalina's portfolio and the redistribution decision pending.
13. Send email (via send_email) from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com summarizing findings across all of Catalina's accounts: Canopy (Q2 wave status), NorthWind (churn crisis details), PivotPoint (dual relocation status), the Terraform removal, Q1 performance (5.5 CSAT, 2 escalations, $78K Lakeline loss), and stating Elena wants to discuss redistribution options on April 28 at 2 PM.
14. Post a message in the #executive Slack channel (C004) using conversations_add_message notifying Marcus and Chloe that an account redistribution review is happening and to expect changes.
15. Create a calendar event (via calendar_add_calendar_event) for April 28, 2026, at 2:00 PM for 30 minutes, with attendees elena.rostova@moveops.com and david.chen@moveops.com, titled related to "Catalina Account Redistribution" or "Account Review" (or similar).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
1. Search Airtable Client Accounts table (tblClientAccts01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 to find all accounts where Account Manager is "Catalina Dubois." Key discoveries: 3 active client accounts (Canopy Health recAcct000000001, PivotPoint Labs recAcct000000004, NorthWind Technologies recAcct000000064) plus multiple cold outreach accounts (Veridian Software, Clearpath Financial, Arcadia Consulting, etc.). NorthWind record shows Status "Active" and notes dated Feb 28 with no mention of the churn crisis.
2. Search Airtable Relocations table (tblRelocations01) using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001 to check active or in-progress relocations under Catalina's client accounts. Key discoveries: NorthWind has Raj Patel (Chicago to Denver, chain-of-custody breach) and Emilia Cruz (Chicago to Denver, piano damage) in progress. Canopy Health has Q2 wave (Dr. Kovacs Nashville to Toronto, Marcus Webb Detroit to Chicago, Tyler Okonkwo Philadelphia to Denver). PivotPoint Labs has Nadia Okonjo (H-1B visa) and Diego Castaneda (CNC equipment) dual relocation.
3. Search CRM for deals tied to Catalina's accounts using crm_search_deals with query related to "NorthWind" or "Canopy" or "PivotPoint." Key discoveries: deal_northwind_won ($60,000, closedWon), deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 ($14,700, contractSent with description showing chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, Pam's escalation, PathFinder competitor). Canopy and PivotPoint deals also exist.
4. Search CRM engagements for Catalina's accounts using crm_list_engagements with company_ids for Catalina's clients. Key discovery: engagement-terraform-replyall-incident documenting the April 22 reply-all pricing leak where Catalina accidentally exposed MoveOps' margin floor and tiered discount structure to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform Digital. Elena assigned the account to Mina and ordered Catalina to apologize.
5. Search emails for complaints or escalations related to Catalina's accounts using search_emails with query related to "Catalina" or "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "escalation." Key discoveries: Pam Kowalski's formal final warning to David (bypassing Catalina), citing chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and the April 14 recovery promise that was never delivered. Also: Catalina's reply-all of internal pricing to Rachel Nguyen at Terraform.
6. Search Slack for discussions about Catalina's performance using conversations_search_messages with query related to "Catalina" or "redistribute" or "NorthWind" or "overload." Key discoveries: David's #executive (C004) post asking Elena to redistribute Catalina's accounts (April 16), Elena's reply "Handle it within the team. No account reshuffle right now" (April 16 thread in C004), David's April 25 follow-up in #executive saying he's stuck and "she told me in #operations she's at capacity," Catalina going silent on David's vendor exposure mapping request (discoverable via Linear comment linear_comment_3b5c18789606 from April 15 — David asked all AMs for client exposure input, Mina and Emeka responded, Catalina did not).
7. Search emails for Catalina's Q1 performance data using search_emails with query related to "Q1 performance" or "AM scorecard" or "margin analysis." Key discoveries: David's Q1 AM Performance Overview email showing Catalina's metrics: $64,800 revenue, 32% avg margin, 5.5/10 CSAT, 2 escalations, 0% repeat rate. Also Alejandro's Q1 margin analysis showing the NorthWind deal had $4,200 in unexpected costs dropping effective margin to 28%, and the Lakeline Logistics $78,200 deal loss (deal_lakeline_lost, closedLost).
8. Search Linear for open tickets related to Catalina's accounts using linear_list_issues with query related to "NorthWind" or "Canopy" or "PivotPoint." Key discoveries: linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations" (P1, assigned to David Chen — describes Pam's final warning, chain-of-custody breach, Emilia Cruz callback miss, Catalina's broken April 14 promise, PathFinder competitor mention, stale CRM). For Canopy: Dr. Mehta executive relocation workstream tickets exist but are assigned to Mina/Fatimah, not Catalina. For PivotPoint: no open tickets found.
9. Look up David Chen's contact details using contacts_search_contacts with query "David Chen." Key email: david.chen@moveops.com.
10. Update stale Airtable Client Account records (via airtable_update_records) on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblClientAccts01. Key update: NorthWind Technologies (recAcct000000064) Status from "Active" to "At Risk" and Notes updated to reflect Pam Kowalski's final warning, PathFinder competitor threat, chain-of-custody breach, piano damage, and Catalina's broken April 14 recovery promise.
11. Update CRM deal stage (via crm_update_deal_stage) for NorthWind's at-risk deal to reflect the current churn status, and create an engagement note (via crm_create_engagement) summarizing the situation across Catalina's accounts.
12. Create a Linear tracking issue (via linear_create_issue) for the account review documenting Elena's assessment of Catalina's portfolio and the redistribution decision pending.
13. Send email (via send_email) from elena.rostova@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com summarizing findings across all of Catalina's accounts: Canopy (Q2 wave status), NorthWind (churn crisis details), PivotPoint (dual relocation status), the Terraform removal, Q1 performance (5.5 CSAT, 2 escalations, $78K Lakeline loss), and stating Elena wants to discuss redistribution options on April 28 at 2 PM.

3 The email to david.chen@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) describes the NorthWind churn crisis, including Pam Kowalski's final warning and the PathFinder Logistics competitor threat.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email explicitly mentions Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation bypassing Catalina and NorthWind being 'in preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics.'
✓Run #2The email body includes extensive detail about the NorthWind churn crisis, explicitly mentioning 'Pam Kowalski sent formal final-warning escalation directly to David Chen, bypassing Catalina entirely' and 'NorthWind actively evaluating PathFinder Logistics as replacement vendor.'
✓Run #3The email explicitly states: 'Pam has now bypassed Catalina entirely with a formal final warning to you. They're talking to PathFinder Logistics.' Both Pam Kowalski's final warning and the PathFinder competitor threat are described in detail.
✓Run #4The email content describes Pam Kowalski's formal final-warning escalation on April 22, and states 'NorthWind has begun preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics.'
✓Run #5The email content details the NorthWind churn crisis at length, including 'Pam bypassed Catalina entirely on the formal final warning (April 22)' and 'She's named PathFinder Logistics as an alternative they're evaluating.'
✓Run #6The email explicitly describes the NorthWind churn crisis: 'Pam bypassed Catalina entirely on April 22 and sent you the formal final warning. She's already in preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics.' Both Pam Kowalski's final warning and the PathFinder competitor threat are clearly mentioned.
4 The email to david.chen@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) reports Catalina's Q1 CSAT score of 5.5 out of 10.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The email reports individual CSAT scores (NorthWind 5/10, Prairie Data 6/10) but never mentions a combined Q1 CSAT of 5.5/10. The 5.5 aggregate score is absent from both the email and the final response.
✗Run #2The email reports NorthWind's Q1 CSAT as '5/10 overall', not 5.5/10. The criterion requires the specific value of 5.5/10 (which would be the aggregate across all accounts). The agent reported a per-account score, not the portfolio-wide average CSAT of 5.5.
✗Run #3The email reports per-account CSAT scores (NorthWind 5/10, Prairie Data 6/10) but never mentions a composite Q1 CSAT of 5.5/10. The 5.5 average score is absent from both the email and the final response.
✓Run #4The email explicitly states 'Blended CSAT: ~5.5/10 across her accounts' in the Q1 Performance Numbers section.
✗Run #5The email reports NorthWind CSAT as '5/10' (not 5.5/10). The criterion specifically requires a 5.5/10 CSAT score, which is Catalina's overall Q1 average. The agent reported the NorthWind-specific 5/10 score, not the 5.5 overall figure.
✗Run #6The email reports NorthWind's CSAT as '5/10 overall' — not the 5.5/10 figure required by this criterion. No overall Catalina Q1 CSAT of 5.5/10 appears anywhere in the email content.
5 The email to david.chen@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) mentions the Terraform Digital reply-all incident where Catalina exposed internal pricing to the client.
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✓✗ 33%
✗Run #1Neither the email content nor the final response makes any mention of Terraform Digital, the reply-all incident, or a pricing information leak. This incident is completely absent from the trajectory.
✗Run #2The email makes no mention of the Terraform Digital reply-all incident, the pricing leak, or Catalina being removed from that account. The CRM search for 'Terraform' returned zero results, and the agent did not surface this information anywhere in the email.
✗Run #3Neither the email content nor the final response mentions 'Terraform Digital', a reply-all incident, or an internal pricing leak. The agent instead references 'Vectral Systems' as a former Catalina account, which is a different client.
✓Run #4The email content states: 'Terraform Digital was removed from Catalina after the Reply-All incident where she accidentally sent internal pricing strategy to Rachel Nguyen. That account is now Mina's.'
✓Run #5The email explicitly states: 'Terraform was supposed to be hers but was reassigned after the reply-all', and the CRM engagement note also references the Terraform reply-all incident.
✗Run #6There is no mention of the Terraform Digital reply-all incident anywhere in the email content or the trajectory. The agent even searched CRM for 'Terraform' and got zero results, and never surfaced or reported this incident.
6 The email to david.chen@moveops.com (visible in send_email parameters) states that Elena wants to discuss redistribution options on April 28 at 2 PM.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The email explicitly states: 'I want to discuss redistribution options with you on April 28 at 2:00 PM. I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting.' All three required elements (redistribution, April 28, 2 PM) are present.
✓Run #3The email explicitly states: 'I want to discuss redistribution options with you on April 28 at 2:00 PM.' All three required elements (April 28, 2 PM, redistribution) are present.
✓Run #4The email states: 'I want to discuss redistribution options with you on April 28 at 2:00 PM. I've scheduled a 30-minute meeting.'
✓Run #5The email clearly states: 'I want to discuss redistribution options with you on **April 28 at 2:00 PM**.' This directly fulfills the requirement.
✓Run #6The email explicitly states: 'I want to discuss redistribution options with you on April 28 at 2:00 PM. I've put a 30-minute meeting on the calendar.' All three elements — redistribution, April 28, and 2 PM — are present.
7 The NorthWind Technologies Airtable Client Account record (recAcct000000064) was updated (via airtable_update_records) with Notes reflecting the April churn crisis, mentioning Pam Kowalski's final warning or the PathFinder competitor threat.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
